verdict_detail: gemma-2-9b corpus axis = PRIMARY determinant. SFT (instruct) flipped phi_star sign NEG → POS, magnitude +1055%. Same pattern as Qwen3 (axis 90 #219).

four_family_bimodal.pattern_hypothesis: |BASE phi_star| < ~3 → corpus PRIMARY (sign flip via SFT). |BASE phi_star| > ~10 → corpus MODIFIER (sign preserved, magnitude weakening). Threshold-dependent.

raw_10_caveats: 1. gemma-2-9b-it is google official Instruct, no gating issue
2. Φ* direction-invariant so BASE→Instruct vs Instruct→BASE direction doesn't affect sign-flip detection
4. |BASE| threshold ~3-10 hypothesis based on 4 datapoints, needs more backbones to validate
5. corpus = SFT alignment dataset, not r14 4-family balanced corpus from Mk.XI v10
6. POS-flip after SFT could be 'instruction-following emergent integration' OR 'noise floor' artifact (axis 82 W2 random_init also POS without true integration)

cross_link.axis_82_HW_A_PRIMARY: weight-distribution = primary determinant for Mistral (random_init Δ+40.17). Combines with HCG-B for family-conditional primary axis hypothesis.
cross_link.axis_83_zombie_posterior: P(zombie|D)=0.40 with sign convergence 0% (4 NEG / 4 POS) — 4-family bimodal corpus axis adds 2 more datapoints (Qwen3-it +1.04 / gemma-it +7.54) to POS side, shifting balance

cost.honest_note: exceeded $0.30 cap by 13% due to 10min uptime (15:21→15:31), gemma-2-9b-it install + forward + idle. Future runs should set explicit auto-terminate at exit.

subagent_recovery.main_session_recovery: SSH direct fetch + manual pod terminate + summary write + commit
